skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automates Twitter/X posting, engagement, direct messages, follows, and profile lookup through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Run Twitter/X account actions through inference.sh app commands.
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: Static external command and network findings are confirmed as real capability risks because the skill grants Bash access to infsh and documents account-changing X/Twitter operations. No prompt injection, credential theft, or malicious exfiltration intent was found in SKILL.md. The static weak cryptography blocker findings at lines 3 and 27 are false positives from descriptive text, not cryptographic code.

medium_findings.title: Broad infsh Command Permission
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access to infsh commands and provides many executable examples. This is expected for the skill, but it allows external service calls and account actions through the inference.sh CLI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples directly show infsh execution. The risk is clear, but the commands match the stated purpose and do not show malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Account-Changing Social Automation
medium_findings.description: The documented apps can post, like, retweet, delete posts, send direct messages, and follow users. These actions can affect a real X/Twitter account and can be misused for spam or unwanted engagement.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The app list and examples clearly describe account-changing operations. Legitimate social automation is possible, so this is a medium risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Expected External URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh, hosted example media, documentation pages, or a user-provided media placeholder. These are network dependencies, but no evidence of covert data exfiltration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation, image, and placeholder links related to the advertised service. There is no hidden endpoint or secret-bearing request in the file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptography alerts at lines 3 and 27 do not correspond to cryptographic algorithms. They appear to match descriptive terms such as X API and App ID in Markdown content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is Markdown documentation and contains no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation at those lines. The static blocker is not supported by semantic context.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on Bash execution of infsh app commands. Users should review every proposed command before it runs because commands can interact with external services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in the allowed-tools metadata and command examples. The risk is inherent to executing a networked CLI.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated Public and Private Engagement
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples include posting, liking, retweeting, direct messages, and following users. These operations can create public content or private messages from a connected account.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly document account-changing commands. The concern is misuse or accidental execution, not hidden malicious code.

content.value_statement: Managing social posts and engagement across X can be repetitive and error-prone. This skill prepares inference.sh commands for posting, media updates, messages, follows, and profile checks.
actual_capabilities: Prepare infsh commands to post text updates to X/Twitter.
Create posts with media URLs through the x/post-create app.
Like, retweet, delete, and retrieve posts by tweet ID.
Send direct messages when a recipient ID is provided.
Follow users and retrieve user profiles by username.
Chain media generation apps with X posting workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Does not include built-in rate limit checks or approval workflows.
Cannot verify X policy compliance before a command runs.
Media posting depends on reachable media URLs from external services.

use_cases.title: Publish Campaign Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare repeatable post commands for announcements, launches, and scheduled content batches.

use_cases.title: Support Account Engagement
use_cases.description: Retrieve profiles, inspect posts, and prepare replies or direct messages for approved support workflows.

use_cases.title: Share Generated Media
use_cases.description: Connect image or video generation outputs to media posts on X using inference.sh apps.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an infsh command to post this text to X: [message]. Show the command and ask for confirmation before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Media Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Post with an existing media URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a media post for X with this caption: [caption] and this media URL: [url]. Include the required infsh app and input fields.

prompt_templates.title: Review Engagement Actions
prompt_templates.scenario: Controlled like, retweet, or follow action
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare safe commands for these approved engagement actions: [tweet IDs or usernames]. Separate each action and explain its account impact.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Generated Media Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an inference.sh workflow that generates [image or video topic], captures the media URL, and prepares an X post with this caption: [caption].

output_examples.input: A product marketer wants to announce a feature release on X.
output_examples.output: A ready-to-review posting command, a short confirmation note, and a reminder that the command publishes from the connected account.

output_examples.input: A community manager needs to follow one user and like two approved posts.
output_examples.output: A separated action plan with one follow command and two like commands, each tied to the provided usernames or tweet IDs.

output_examples.input: A creator has a hosted image URL and wants to publish it with a caption.
output_examples.output: A media post command using the provided image URL, plus a note to verify that the URL is public before publishing.

best_practices: Review every generated command before execution because actions can publish content or send messages.
Use approved account credentials and keep inference.sh authentication separate from shared workspaces.
Check X platform rules, rate limits, and brand approval before running engagement automation.
anti_patterns: Do not automate unsolicited direct messages or mass follows.
Do not run generated commands that include unknown tweet IDs, usernames, or media URLs.
Do not use the skill from an account that lacks permission to publish the planned content.

faq.question: Does this skill post directly to X?
faq.answer: It prepares and can run inference.sh CLI commands that post through a connected X integration.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: You need the infsh CLI, an inference.sh login, and a configured X integration.

faq.question: Can it post media?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents media posting when you provide a reachable media URL.

faq.question: Can it send direct messages?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an x/dm-send workflow that requires a recipient ID and message text.

faq.question: Is this safe for fully automated engagement?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill can perform real account actions, so human approval is recommended.

faq.question: Does it store credentials?
faq.answer: No evidence of credential storage was found in SKILL.md. Authentication is handled by the external inference.sh CLI.
